mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 200k persons signals robust labor market demand and future consumption capacity. This drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings expectations upward in response to strengthening employment momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 144894k as of May 2021 shows strong post-pandemic recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with accelerating labor demand
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS month-over-month change versus 20-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or payroll growth acceleration does not precede positive returns by 5-15 trading days in >55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year, real rates turn negative. This negative real rate regime compresses equity risk premiums and drives capital away from bonds into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.06% and CPIAUCSL at 268.551 as of May 2021 indicate negative real rates
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of triggered instances despite negative real rate signal, or the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) of more than 1% signals weakening consumer demand and future earnings headwinds. This triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors immediately reprice equity valuations downward in anticipation of demand destruction.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 620214M as of May 2021 reflects post-pandemic demand normalization
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >55% of triggered instances despite negative demand signal, or Rank IC of RSAFS MoM change versus 5-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) remains elevated above 5.5%, signaling supply-side weakness combined with slack labor markets. This regime produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors recognize stagflationary pressures and margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.8562 and UNRATE at 5.8% as of May 2021
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite supply-labor weakness signal, or the interaction term (INDPRO × UNRATE) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1600k units annualized, signaling strong residential investment demand and forward-looking builder confidence. This demand signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through multiplier effects on construction employment, materials demand, and consumer wealth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1569k units as of April 2021 shows strong residential demand
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with robust housing demand
failure_condition: Rank IC of HOUST level versus 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or housing acceleration does not precede positive returns by 10-30 trading days in >55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate of more than 0.25 percentage points signals the onset of monetary tightening. This tightening cycle produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice equity discount rates upward and reduce growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.06% as of May 2021 provides room for tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite rate increase signal, or Rank IC of FEDFUNDS MoM change versus 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month, it signals an abrupt deterioration in labor market conditions. This shock produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors immediately reprice recession risk and earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.8% as of May 2021 reflects ongoing labor market recovery
regime_hint: labor market shock or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >55% of triggered instances despite unemployment spike signal, or Rank IC of UNRATE MoM change versus 5-day forward returns is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously grow above 1% month-over-month, signaling synchronized demand expansion across both production and consumption. This dual demand signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors recognize broad-based economic acceleration and earnings upside.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized change > 2.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month change > 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19088.064B and RSAFS at 620214M as of Q1 2021 show strong demand
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion in late-cycle recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of triggered instances despite dual demand acceleration signal, or the interaction term (GDPC1 × RSAFS) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
